# Transient Introduction

Given a coil with z=200 turns and L=1 mH inductance. find the inductance L', in mH, after adding 0.2z turns: so that the total is z'=(1+0.2)z

$L'=\left( \frac{Z'}{Z} \right)^{2}·L=1.2^{2}·L$
$L'=1.44mH$

$u=L \frac{di}{dt}$
$t=\frac{L}{L}\int u \ dt + i(0)$


---
A switch is thrown at t=0. Compute the energy sored in $L_{1}$, at the steady state condition, in mJ. Given E=2V, R=4Ω, $L_{1}=4mH$, $L_{2}=7mH$

$i_{s}(5\pi)=\frac{E}{R}=0.5A$

$W_{L_{1}}=\frac{L_{1}i_{s}^{2}(5\pi)}{2}=0.5mJ$

---

$\boxed{i(0_{-})=i(0_{+})}$

---

If the curret in 10mH coil changes as shown in the figure, find its voltage (sign and value,in mV) at $t=t_{2}$. Given $I^{+}=6mA$, $I^{-}=6mA$, $t_{1}=2ms$,$t_{3}=4t_{1}$

$0<t<t_{1}$
$u=10·10^{-3} \frac{6·10^{-3}}{2·10^{-3z}}=30mV$
$t_{1}\leq t\leq t_{3}$
$u=10·\cancel{ 10^{-3} } \frac{(-8·10^{-3})-6·10^{-3}}{3·2·\cancel{ 10^{-3} }}=-23.3mV$

---

# Transient 1st Order Circuits
after 5 time constants everything is stable
![[Pasted image 20251216130649.png]]

$@T=\frac{L}{R_{t}}=\frac{L}{R_{W}+R_{L}}$

# 13.01.26 

$T=R_{e}C$
$T=\frac{L}{R_{e}}$

$t>5T$
$i_{2}(5T)=\frac{E}{2R}$
$i(0_{+})=\frac{5}{3} \frac{E}{R}$
$i_{2}(5T)=\frac{E}{2R}$
$i(5T)=i_{1}(T)+i_{2}(T)=\frac{3E}{2R}$

# 20.01.26
## S-domain analysis
https://platforma.polsl.pl/rau3/pluginfile.php/121685/mod_resource/content/0/IEEC_1_Lecture_10_Transient_1st_Order_S_Domain%28Students%29.pdf

| f(t)                        | F(s)                         |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| $\mathbb{1}(t)$             | $\frac{1}{s}$                |
| $\delta(t)$                 | $\frac{1}{T}$                |
| $e^{-t/T}\mathbb{1}(t)$     | $\frac{T}{s(1+sT)}$          |
| $(1-e^{-t/T})\mathbb{1}(t)$ | $\frac{\mathbb{1}}{s(1+sT)}$ |
$Z_{c}=\frac{1}{sc}$
$Z_{L}=sL$

Ohm's law in s-domain
$U(s)=Z(s)·I(s)$

$Z(s)=\frac{U(s)}{I(s)}$ S-domain impedance
$Y(s)=\frac{1}{Z(s)}=\frac{I(s)}{U(s)}$

2) Impulse function
$Y(s)=(\frac{E}{S}-\frac{E}{S}e^{ -st_{0} }) \frac{1}{1+sRc}$
$y(t)=E(1-e^{-t/T})\mathbb{1}(t)- E\left( 1-e^{-\frac{t-t_{0}}{T}} \right)\mathbb{1}(t-t_{0})$

convert
$I(s)=\frac{E}{s}·\frac{1}{sL+R+\frac{1}{sC}}$
to
$F(s)=\frac{N(s)}{sD(s)}$
to
$f(t)=\frac{N(0)}{D(0)}+\sum^\infty_{k=1} \frac{n(sk)}{s_{k}D(s_{k})}$

keeping everything in a single note makes my laptop sad
